name: Joomla
description: Popular open source CMS with built-in ACL, multilingual support, and extensive extension ecosystem

description.value: Popular open source CMS with built-in ACL, multilingual support, and extensive extension ecosystem

initial-release.comment: Joomla 1.0.0 released September 16, 2005, forked from Mambo

cms-type.comment: Traditional with PHP templates, plus headless capability via Web Services API (REST) since Joomla 4

deployment-model.comment: Self-hosted only; no official managed/SaaS offering from Joomla project

database-support.comment: MySQL/MariaDB primary; PostgreSQL supported since Joomla 3.0

api-type.comment: Web Services API (REST) in core since Joomla 4; GraphQL only via third-party extensions/middleware

multi-tenancy.comment: No built-in multisite; each site requires separate installation

static-site-generation.comment: No native SSG; can be used as headless backend for Gatsby/Next.js via REST API

edge-deployment.comment: PHP-based; requires traditional server, though content can be cached via CDN

content-types.comment: Articles, categories, contacts, newsfeeds in core; custom content types via extensions like K2, CCK, or custom components

custom-fields.comment: 15 different field types built into core since Joomla 3.7

field-types.comment: Core includes text, editor, media, list, radio, checkboxes, calendar, integer, user, etc.; repeater via subform field

content-relationships.comment: Related articles feature in core; references via custom fields

nested-components.comment: Subform field type allows nested/repeating field groups

localization.comment: Over 70 translation packs available; built-in multilingual content management with language associations

content-validation.comment: Field validation rules available for custom fields

embedded-interactives.comment: Modules can be embedded in content; custom HTML modules for widgets

content-versioning.comment: Content versioning prevents loss of previous article versions

draft-publish.comment: Publishing Workflow feature introduced in Joomla 4 with customizable states

scheduled-publishing.comment: Choose when articles are published/unpublished with start and finish dates

approval-workflows.comment: Publishing Workflow component in Joomla 4+ with custom states and transitions

content-locking.comment: Check-in/check-out system in core prevents concurrent editing

audit-logging.comment: Action Logs component in core tracks user actions

media-library.comment: Media Manager built into core for uploading and managing images

image-transformations.comment: Image resizing and cropping in Media Manager; additional transforms via extensions

external-media-storage.comment: Third-party extensions available for S3, Cloudinary integration

video-support.comment: Media Manager supports video uploads; YouTube/Vimeo embedding in editor

svg-support.comment: SVG support configurable in Media Manager settings

folder-organization.comment: Media Manager supports folder creation and organization

role-based-access.comment: Integrated ACL (Access Control List) with user groups and viewing access levels

granular-permissions.comment: ACL allows granular access rights for different user types at component, category, and article level

sso-saml.comment: SSO/SAML via third-party extensions like miniOrange SAML SSO

team-collaboration.comment: Workflow notifications via core messaging; assignment through user groups

audience-restrictions.comment: Access levels control which user groups can view content

typescript-sdk.comment: No official TypeScript/JavaScript SDK; developers use standard HTTP clients with REST API

content-type-sdk.comment: Content types defined via PHP component development or admin UI; no code-first approach like Strapi

cli-tools.comment: Joomla Console CLI with ~30 commands for cache, config, database, extensions, users, updates

local-development.comment: Standard PHP development with XAMPP, MAMP, Docker, or similar

webhooks.comment: No native webhooks; available via third-party extensions like Content Sharing - Webhooks

plugin-system.comment: Extensive extension ecosystem: components, modules, plugins, templates, languages

admin-ui-customization.comment: Admin templates customizable; Cassiopeia admin template in Joomla 4+

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate documentation with wiki and manual; undergoing modernization; developer docs at manual.joomla.org

frontend-frameworks.comment: No official framework integrations; can work headlessly with any framework via REST API

search-integration.comment: Smart Search (com_finder) in core with full-text indexing

preview-mode.comment: Frontend editing and preview capability in core

ecommerce-integration.comment: E-commerce via extensions like VirtueMart, J2Store, HikaShop

analytics-integration.comment: Google Analytics and other analytics via extensions

ai-features.comment: No native AI features; available via third-party extensions

cdn-included.comment: Self-hosted; CDN integration via hosting provider or manually

response-caching.comment: Cache management built into core to speed up website

free-tier.comment: Fully open source and free to use

pricing-model.comment: Core software is free; commercial extensions and hosting are optional

commercial-support.comment: Joomla Service Providers directory lists certified support providers

enterprise-features.comment: ACL, workflows, and multisite capabilities support enterprise needs; SSO via extensions
